card.intuition: A behavioral pattern that turns a set of behaviors into objects and makes them interchangeable inside an original context object.
card.analogy: A versatile gaming mouse: you can swap the side-panels. If you're playing an FPS, you snap on the '2-button' panel (**Strategy A**). If you're playing an MMO, you snap on the '12-button' panel (**Strategy B**). The mouse (Context) stays the same, but its behavior changes instantly.
when_to_use: Providing different sorting algorithms (QuickSort vs BubbleSort) that a user can choose at runtime
Handling different payment methods (PayPal, CreditCard, Crypto) in an e-commerce checkout
Implementing different validation rules for a form based on the country
gotchas: The 'Client' must be aware of the different strategies to choose one. If you have 50 strategies, the client code might get messy just picking which one to use.
It's the OOP version of a 'Switch' statement — but much more flexible and extensible.

exercise.prompt: Identify which SOLID principle the Strategy pattern primarily supports by allowing behaviors to be added without modifying the context class.
